rationale: Both correctly diagnose the same root cause as the thread (default core_model run-level forces load_format:dummy, extended to online serving by #4354; not a real bug, closed invalid) and give the correct --run-level=full_model fix. Y is more grounded, citing more distinct code sites (run_args.py, runtime.py, stage_config.py, assertions.py) and adding the non-obvious point that pytest.mark.full_model is just a selection marker independent of --run-level, which explains why node-ID selection still defaults to core_model. Y is also more complete, explicitly addressing the reporter's A100 hardware (matching the issue title) versus the H100/MI325 hardware_test decorator, and explaining both failure signatures (repeated 'Joe' text and Welsh-like audio). X's answer is correct but thinner and has a redundant, near-duplicate draft_answer/additional-context structure.
